title: esenbeckia oligantha
intro: Esenbeckia oligantha, also known as Prensa or Prensa de sangre, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial properties, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine. The health benefits of Esenbeckia oligantha include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and act as a natural antiseptic to heal wounds and prevent infections. Herbal preparations of Esenbeckia oligantha typically include tea infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from digestive issues to skin conditions.
